excerpt: The world is a house in which sins abound; after death, only deeds remain, and in the grave one regrets deeply.
source_location_hint: Early Fatimid ethical teaching on repentance and afterlife

excerpt: Remembrance of Allah elevates the soul and weakens Satan; recitation of the Qur'an with reflection moves the heart to fear and longing.
source_location_hint: Teaching on the three fortresses of faith

excerpt: The perfection of faith is realized only through jihād and culminates in the wilāyat of ʿAlī and his descendants.
source_location_hint: On the completion of faith through loyalty to the Imāms

excerpt: Intellect is the jewel that renders humans the noblest of created things; its true nobility lies in exercising it in belief, intention, knowledge, and deed.
source_location_hint: On the nobility of intellect and its actualization

excerpt: Envy consumes one's religion and leads to destruction, as Qābīl's envy of Hābīl led him to murder and ruin.
source_location_hint: On the spiritual consequences of envy

excerpt: Backbiting is a truth that should not be uttered; the listener is an equal partner in the sin and the nullification of deeds.
source_author: Attributed to Imam ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_authority: High confidence (canonical Shiite source, ethical teaching)
source_location_hint: Sayings on the gravity of backbiting

excerpt: The heart engaged in the world inherits misery, humiliation, and calamity; the heart engaged in the Hereafter inherits Paradise; the heart engaged in the Divine attains religion.
source_location_hint: Teaching on the three stations of the heart

excerpt: Jihād in the way of Allah signifies striving to attain knowledge of His Oneness and to comprehend the Divine limits and one's own station.
source_authority: High confidence (Ismaili taʾwīl of Qur'anic terms)

excerpt: ʿAlī's lament over Fāṭima: every companionship dissolves in death; no companion endures; even the Prophet and his successor must be parted.
source_author: Attributed to Imam ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_authority: High confidence (canonical Shiite elegy and testimony)
source_location_hint: Verses at the burial of Fāṭima, daughter of the Prophet
